_comment: Projects in this file are clone-able anonymously and are run by the manual CI job `pages:regression`.
Each entry has to be a Rell project with valid chromia.yml
name             — workdir directory + report row label (required)
ref              — branch / tag / commit; omit for repo's default branch
rellPath         — directory containing chromia.yml, relative to repo root (default: ".")
commands         — list of chr invocations to run sequentially; non-zero exit short-circuits.
Default: [["install"], ["build"]]. Set [["install"]] for libs that aren't meant to be
expectedFailure  — known-bad against verified Rell builds
notes            — free-form, rendered in the report

projects.name: ft4-lib
projects.notes: Chromia FT4 token-fungibles library; reference codebase for the perf module's bench samples.

projects.name: postchain-eif
projects.notes: Postchain Ethereum Interoperability Framework. chromia.yml lives under postchain-eif-rell/rell/.

projects.name: ft3-lib
projects.notes: Predecessor of FT4. Currently passes install — left as-is to surface future regressions.

projects.name: lib-pagination-utils
projects.notes: Pagination helper library; chromia.yml lives under rell/. chromia.yml's `module: core` points at a non-existent module (modules live at src/core/pagination/), and that misconfiguration is present on every commit since the file was introduced — so `chr build` has never worked here. Run install only; the project is consumed as a library by downstream chains.

projects.name: originals-governance
projects.notes: Chromia Originals governance dapp; chromia.yml lives under rell/. lib-pagination-utils is referenced via ssh://git@bitbucket.org/... — CI runners have no Bitbucket SSH host key (and no account key to authenticate with), so we rewrite the registry to HTTPS pre-install. The library is public so anonymous HTTPS clones work.
